summary: Completed backend+frontend regression for notification workflows, calibration heatmap, and core review/export/settings flows. Backend notification and analytics APIs passed; UI confirmed notification center behavior, owner notifications, crew follow-up visibility, heatmap rendering, and settings blueprint/roadmap loading.
action_items: Optional: add explicit loading-state wait in UI tests before asserting crew follow-up card visibility, since notification fetch is async.
Optional maintainability refactor: split backend/server.py (>1400 lines) into focused modules (auth, submissions, reviews, analytics, exports, notifications).
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is significantly over 700 lines, increasing regression risk and review complexity; module split is recommended.
seed_data_creation: No manual seed scripts created. Test data generated through application APIs/workflows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Crew follow-up card was verified visible on /crew/000a07ca after allowing async load. Owner queue item click and owner form opening were verified. Backend notification and heatmap regression suite is reusable in test_notifications_and_heatmap.py.
rca of the issue: No blocking production issue reproduced in this iteration. Earlier transient UI warning on crew follow-up visibility was timing-related (async fetch), not backend notification generation failure.
